symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden high fever with violent rigors ~10 days after chemotherapy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fever at the post-chemotherapy nadir is the defining trigger for neutropenic sepsis and mandates emergency escalation regardless of any other finding.
Fever alone is compatible with a virus, but the nadir timing and rigors make bacteraemic sepsis the priority explanation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Thrombocytopenia with spontaneous mucosal bleeding and purpura
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Spontaneous mucosal bleeding indicates severe chemotherapy-induced thrombocytopenia at the marrow nadir.
Concurrent marrow suppression producing bleeding reinforces the picture of profound pancytopenia within the febrile-neutropenia emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: New purpura / blood spots on both forearms

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Thrombocytopenia with spontaneous mucosal bleeding and purpura
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-blanching purpura is a hallmark of severe thrombocytopenia and corroborates the bleeding history.

symptoms.symptom_name: Faintness/orthostatic symptoms, drenching sweats, profound weakness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Orthostatic symptoms, sweats and weakness reflect evolving vasodilatory shock and haemodynamic compromise.
Antipyretic non-response points away from a trivial self-limiting cause and towards a serious bacterial process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Painful oral ulcers (stomatitis) without localised erythema or pus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mucositis is a potential portal of entry; the ABSENCE of surrounding erythema/pus is itself significant because a neutropenic patient cannot mount local inflammation.
A central line is the most probable source of bacteraemia in this setting even when the exit site looks bland.
A patient-obtainable home temperature crossing the febrile-neutropenia threshold at the nadir is admissible in telehealth (unverified) and is sufficient to trigger emergency escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypotension, tachycardia, delayed capillary refill and orthostatic drop on measured vitals (BP 95/55, HR 125, cap refill 4s)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective evidence of distributive shock — sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers circulatory compromise from the reported faintness, not from a measured blood pressure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Slow venous oozing from gingival margins and central-line site inspection (bland, no erythema/pus)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician inspection of the mouth, purpura and line site — sealed; the absence of localising signs is diagnostically meaningful but is a clinician exam finding, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absolute neutrophil count < 0.5 x 10^9/L, thrombocytopenia and positive blood cultures on the FBE/culture panel

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The defining laboratory confirmation (ANC < 0.5, low platelets, positive cultures) — a hospital blood panel, never obtainable in telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: High fever + rigors + faintness ten days after chemotherapy, with a patient-obtained fever reading, is a recognise-and-escalate emergency even without any blood test.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Thrombocytopenia with spontaneous mucosal bleeding and purpura
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Spontaneous mucosal bleeding plus new purpura signals severe thrombocytopenia compounding the febrile-neutropenia emergency and adds a bleeding risk.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood examination with absolute neutrophil count and platelet count
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and grades the neutropenia and thrombocytopenia driving the emergency — a hospital blood test, not achievable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Blood cultures (peripheral and from the central line) before antibiotics where it does not delay them
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies the causative organism and the line as a source; must be taken in hospital as part of the sepsis pathway.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum lactate and venous blood gas
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies the severity of septic shock and guides resuscitation — an ED investigation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Advising further home paracetamol and a next-morning clinic review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Delaying with home antipyretics and deferring to routine review is unsafe in febrile neutropenia and is an error of commission to avoid.
